codeReview.summary: HTTP service using Effect.ts with Either for error handling has been implemented correctly. The implementation includes HttpClient with GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH methods, error type mapping, ValidatedHttpClient with Effect Schema validation, and createApiClient factory for type-safe API endpoints. All 14 HTTP-specific tests pass and cover success and error scenarios.

issues.description: ValidatedHttpClient returns string[] for validation errors, which is inconsistent with the HttpError type pattern. Consider creating a ValidationError type to match.

Missing explicit test coverage for ValidatedHttpClient and createApiClient factory functions. While the underlying HttpClient is well-tested, the validation and factory layers could benefit from direct tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Either type usage follows Effect.ts conventions (Either<A, E> where A is success/Right and E is error/Left). The implementation correctly wraps axios and returns Either results.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: resources/js/lib/http.ts uses succeed and fail from either.ts

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the skill procedure correctly - implemented HttpClient with Either, added error types, created ValidatedHttpClient with schema validation, and wrote comprehensive tests.
Worker successfully integrated Effect.ts with axios, using Effect.Schema for validation. This pattern could be documented for future features.

assertionResults.VAL-HTTP-001: pass - Effect HTTP Client Creation: HttpClient is created using Effect.ts with Either for error handling. Uses succeed (right) and fail (left) from either.ts, and Effect.Schema for validation.
assertionResults.VAL-HTTP-002: pass - HTTP GET Request: HttpClient.get method exists (line 162-175 in http.ts), returns Either<HttpResponse<T>, HttpError>. Tests verify GET returns Right on success and Left on network error.
assertionResults.VAL-HTTP-003: pass - HTTP POST Request: HttpClient.post method exists (line 177-190 in http.ts). Tests verify POST returns Right with 201 status on success.
assertionResults.VAL-HTTP-004: pass - HTTP Error Handling: Error types defined (NETWORK_ERROR, TIMEOUT, CLIENT_ERROR, SERVER_ERROR, VALIDATION_ERROR, UNKNOWN). mapAxiosError function correctly maps axios errors to HttpError types. Tests verify all error types.
assertionResults.VAL-HTTP-005: pass - Response Validation with Schema: ValidatedHttpClient exists with get, post, put, delete methods. Uses Effect.Schema.decodeUnknownEither for runtime validation. Returns Either<T, HttpError | string[]>.
assertionResults.VAL-HTTP-006: pass - Type-safe API Client: createApiClient factory function exists (line 277-340 in http.ts). Provides type-safe endpoint configuration with method, path, and creates typed API client.

summary: The HTTP service implementation using Effect.ts with Either for error handling is complete and functional. All 6 validation assertions pass. Tests (14 HTTP-specific tests) all pass. The only issues are non-blocking: minor type inconsistency in validation error return and lack of explicit tests for ValidatedHttpClient and createApiClient. Implementation quality is good and follows existing codebase patterns.
